claim: A bipartisan 18-month congressional investigation (House Oversight + Energy & Commerce, Dec 2022) found FDA's review of Biogen's Aduhelm 'atypical' and the approval preceded by 'corporate greed' - a reputational headwind over the whole amyloid field and FDA's accelerated-approval credibility.
subject: aducanumab (Aduhelm) / Biogen / FDA process; amyloid class
headwind_tailwind: HEADWIND: amyloid-adjacent programs inherit FDA-scrutiny + credibility scrutiny
source: U.S. House Committee on Oversight (press release)
quote: This report documents the atypical FDA review process and corporate greed that preceded FDA's controversial decision to grant accelerated approval to Aduhelm.
tier_note: oversight.house.gov (.gov) official congressional finding = primary (T1). Spec's 'institutional T3' is for softer statements; an official investigation report is a fact, not mere perception.

claim: The SEC charged Cassava Sciences and two former executives over misleading claims about its Alzheimer's drug simufilam, with a Cassava-affiliated scientist charged for manipulating trial results; the parties paid >$40M (26 Sept 2024) - a landmark CNS data-integrity scandal.
subject: Cassava Sciences / simufilam; Alzheimer's
headwind_tailwind: HEADWIND: heightened skepticism of small-biotech Alzheimer's cognition claims
quote: Cassava-affiliated university scientist also charged for manipulating clinical trial results ... Cassava Sciences, Inc., its founder and former CEO, Remi Barbier, and its former Senior Vice President of Neuroscience, Dr. Lindsay Burns, will pay more than $40 million

claim: Nature retracted the landmark 2006 Lesné et al. paper on Aβ*56 (24 June 2024) - a foundational, ~2,500-citation amyloid paper - after image-manipulation findings; one of the most-cited retractions ever, casting a credibility cloud over the amyloid field.
subject: amyloid hypothesis (Aβ*56); Lesné et al., Nature 2006
headwind_tailwind: HEADWIND: class-credibility cloud (but see card #4 - hypothesis independently supported)
quote: RETRACTED ARTICLE: A specific amyloid-β protein assembly in the brain impairs memory ... This article was retracted on 24 June 2024

claim: EMET assessment (the agent's perception-vs-merit check): the Lesné retraction invalidated one specific claim (Aβ*56), but the broader amyloid hypothesis rests on independent, intact human genetics - all familial early-onset AD mutations (APP/PSEN1/PSEN2) converge on amyloid-β. Reputational damage =/= refuted science.
subject: amyloid hypothesis - independent genetic support
headwind_tailwind: CONTEXT: separates reputational headwind from the underlying science's validity
quote: APP, PSEN1, or PSEN2 mutations were identified in 53 novel AD-EOAD families ... Of the 53 mutation carriers with available cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) biomarkers, 46 (87%) had all three CSF biomarkers-total tau protein (Tau), phospho-tau protein (P-Tau), and amyloid β (Aβ)42-in abnormal ranges.
